summary: On March 21, 2026, the Rubikon Center reported eliminating 83 Ukrainian targets, including personnel, fixed-wing UAVs, and a UAV control point across Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman directions. The Ukrainian Navy used an FPV drone to locate and destroy an enemy boat near Tendrivska Spit, Kherson Oblast, sinking it with occupants aboard. Russian forces downed a Ka-52 near Nadiivka, Donetsk region, with additional drones striking the crew during evacuation. Ukrainian FPV pilots demonstrated tactics such as striking dismounted infantry, targeting soldiers on horseback, and hitting a Russian soldier hiding behind a tree with two simultaneous drone strikes. The "Elka" anti-drone system achieved its first kinetic interception, destroying a Ukrainian "Darts" fixed-wing drone. Meanwhile, Iranian-backed militias launched a fiber-optic FPV drone attack on the US Victory Base in Baghdad, and analysts debated the viability of switching FPV drones to digital video transmission due to reliability concerns in analog systems.
